titel: The Swedish National Audit Agency's report examines the state's efforts to reduce the shipping industry's emissions of harmful substances into the sea.
html:
In this document, the government presents its assessment regarding:

The Swedish National Audit Agency's observations and recommendations in the report "The State's Efforts to Reduce Shipping Emissions of Harmful Substances into the Sea" (RiR 2024:18). The Swedish National Audit Agency reviewed whether the state's efforts to reduce shipping emissions are effective.

The Swedish National Audit Agency states that the marine environment in Swedish waters is heavily impacted, and that good environmental quality is not being achieved. Furthermore, negative consequences for both the environment and society are expected if comprehensive measures are not taken. The Swedish National Audit Agency also emphasizes that shipping contributes to several environmental burdens in the sea. The review focuses on emissions of scrubber water, emissions of cargo residues, and the reception of shipping waste in ports.

The Swedish National Audit Agency's main recommendations to the government are to:

  • As soon as possible, introduce a ban on the discharge of scrubber water in all of Sweden's territorial waters.
  • Review Swedish regulations to ensure they are in compliance with international convention requirements for monitoring and approval of tank cleaning of cargo tanks, and for "proceeding en route" as a basis for allowing the discharge of cargo residues.

The government largely agrees with the Swedish National Audit Agency's recommendations and has taken or is preparing to take measures to address the issues raised by the recommendations. The government also provides comments on the Swedish National Audit Agency's observations and recommendations that relate to government agencies.

With this document, the government considers the Swedish National Audit Agency's report to be finalized.
tldr: The Swedish National Audit Office has reviewed how the state is working to reduce emissions from ships at sea. They recommend that Sweden prohibit certain types of polluted water and improve controls on the discharge of residues from cargo, in order to protect the environment.
lattlast:
The Swedish National Audit Office is an agency that monitors how the state and government operate. They have investigated whether Sweden's efforts to reduce emissions from shipping are effective. The report shows that the environment in Swedish waters is in poor condition, and that significant changes are needed to halt damage to nature.

The audit primarily focuses on three areas: emissions from scrubber water (water used to clean exhaust gases from ships), residues left over after loading, and how waste is handled in ports. The National Audit Office believes that the government should quickly ban the discharge of scrubber water in all Swedish waters.

The government also wants to review the rules for how the cleaning of tanks on ships is inspected and approved. This is important to ensure that Swedish regulations comply with international laws designed to protect the marine environment. The goal is to reduce the amount of harmful substances that end up in our waters.

The government largely agrees with what the National Audit Office says. They have already begun working on amending the rules, or plan to do so, to address the problems highlighted in the report. This concludes the work related to this particular report.
